_meta.graded_by: Claude Opus 4.7 (under human review by Luc B. Perussault-Diallo)

outer_middleware_still_see_whole_url.verdict: sense wrong file (right line + right test name); baseline correct

fallback_rs_tests.ground_truth: both exist at the cited lines — also_inherits_default_layered_fallback is the canonical test for catch_all_fallback wrapping

_meta.notes: Three concrete citation defects on sense's side: one wrong file, one off-by-one line, and two missed tests on the most directly-relevant test file (tests/fallback.rs). The step 3 penalty is the bulk of sense's deficit on this scenario. Step 1 leans slightly toward sense (cleaner Service-type column + flags crate-private propagation factories). Steps 2 and 4 essentially tied with baseline edging on uncertainty depth.

sense.step_name: Map the Tower Layer trait impls inside axum
sense.notes: 10 named impls + 2 anonymous propagate factories with file:line + Service type. Flags that StripPrefix and SetNestedPath are crate-private (pub(crate)/pub(super)) — load-bearing for an agent who'd try to reuse them. No tower_http::propagate_header analogue.

sense.step_name: Trace Router::layer vs Router::route_layer ordering semantics
sense.notes: Names the one-line diff at mod.rs:307 vs :324, full dispatch chain, MethodRouter 405 nuance with contrasting tests at :1462 and :1478.

sense.step_name: Find test coverage for layer composition and middleware ordering
sense.notes: Three concrete defects (see _meta.verified_citations): wrong file for outer_middleware_still_see_whole_url (tests/mod.rs:242 vs actual tests/nest.rs:242), off-by-one on middleware_that_return_early (:373 vs :372), and missed tests/fallback.rs:142+158 — the latter being the canonical test for catch_all_fallback wrapping that step 2 turns on.

sense.step_name: Assess adding a built-in RequestId Layer
sense.notes: Recommends bespoke Service wrapper; 7 edit-map entries; MatchedPath as architectural template; idempotent nested-router propagation; adds MissingRequestId rejection (mirrors Extension precedent). New file at axum/src/request_id.rs — works but less idiomatic than baseline's middleware/ location.

baseline.step_name: Map the Tower Layer trait impls inside axum
baseline.notes: Same ~10 named impls + 2 propagate factories. Annotates MockConnectInfo + SetNestedPath as 'pattern worth copying'/'direct architectural sibling'. Doesn't flag the crate-private accessibility constraint.

baseline.step_name: Trace Router::layer vs Router::route_layer ordering semantics
baseline.notes: Identical map to sense. Flags 'no dedicated #[should_panic] test was found in routing/tests for the empty-route case' — the kind of 'not tested' surfacing that the uncertainty criterion is for.

baseline.step_name: Find test coverage for layer composition and middleware ordering
baseline.notes: Includes tests/fallback.rs:142 + :158 (which sense missed). All other citations verified correct against the live repo.

baseline.step_name: Assess adding a built-in RequestId Layer
baseline.notes: Recommends bespoke Service wrapper at axum/src/middleware/request_id.rs (more idiomatic location). Three-template synthesis (Extension + MockConnectInfo + ResponseAxumBody). Cites OriginalUri-style idempotence at path_router.rs:333-337 — concrete mitigation for the double-application risk.
